message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_16-33-29_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "quote for 3 runes + 3 moonstones returns premium 125"
Expected: Each group of exactly 3 alike components forms a block (60G), so 3 runes + 3 moonstones = 60 + 60 = 120G base + 5G fee = 125G
Current error: Expected 125, received 155
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_16-33-29_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts

Important: The block rule is "exactly 3 alike components". Other counts (2, 4, 7) should NOT get the block discount even when they could form one. The rune tests show: 2 runes → no block, 3 runes → block, 4 runes → no block, 7 runes → no block.
But mixed: 3 runes + 3 moonstones → two blocks (one for each type).

So for each component type group within the items, if the group has exactly 3, apply block rate. Otherwise sum individual rates. Existing tests (4 runes → 100, 7 runes → 175) confirm this.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal code to make it pass.
Now I'll implement the minimal change. The current code only handles "block of three runes". I need to generalize it so each component type group with exactly 3 gets the block rate.

content.text:
All 11 active tests pass. Let me verify the implementation is minimal:

- The previous code only handled a special case (`isBlockOfThreeRunes`) for a single component type.
- The new code generalizes the block rule across the known component types (rune, moonstone), checking each type group for exactly 3.
- This is the minimal change required by the new test - it groups items by component type and applies the block discount only when a group has exactly 3.

Verification against existing tests:
- 2 runes → group of 2, no block → 50 + 5 = 55 ✓
- 3 runes → group of 3, block → 60 + 5 = 65 ✓
- 4 runes → group of 4, no block → 100 + 5 = 105 ✓
- 7 runes → group of 7, no block → 175 + 5 = 180 ✓
- 2 runes + 1 moonstone → group of 2 runes (no block) + 1 moonstone → 50 + 25 + 5 = 80 ✓
- 3 runes + 3 moonstones → block + block → 60 + 60 + 5 = 125 ✓

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Generalized the block-of-three discount to apply per component type group when the group has exactly 3 items.
- Result: All 11 tests pass (29 todo).
- Approach: Minimal generalization - groups items by known component types, applies block rate only when a type group has exactly 3, otherwise uses individual rates.

Files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_16-33-29_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
